summary: High‑energy early ’80s set with killer Half‑Step > Franklin’s and Ship of Fools
blurb: Opening with a ripping Mississippi Half‑Step → Franklin’s Tower combo sets an electric tone, while the second set delivers a scorching Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain and a heartfelt Ship Of Fools. Highlights also include Black Peter, the inventive 1/2 Step > Franklin’s > Stranger pairing, and tight Don’t Ease Me In → The Promised Land transitions. Recording quality ranges from good soundboard to excellent audience captures, making it a standout early‑‘80s performance.
review:
May 4, 1980 – Baltimore Civic Center

The Dead rode a wave of momentum through the night, launching the first set with Jerry’s soaring lead on “Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo” that segued seamlessly into a jubilant “Franklin’s Tower.” Brent Mydland’s B‑3 work lit up the subsequent “Feel Like A Stranger,” and the band kept the groove rolling through “Althea,” “El Paso,” and a soulful “Brown Eyed Women.” The second set opened with an inspired “Scarlet Begonias” into “Fire On The Mountain,” followed by a beautifully plaintive “Ship Of Fools.” A highlight was the expansive “Playing In The Band” → drums → space excursion that birthed a fiery “Black Peter” and a jubilant “Sugar Magnolia.” The encore’s “U.S. Blues” closed the night with classic Dead energy. Across all recordings, Jerry’s guitar shines, though his vocals crack on the Half‑Step, while Brent’s keyboard solos and Bob’s steady rhythm provide extra spark. With several excellent audience tapes capturing the show in crystal‑clear detail, this concert stands out as a high‑water mark of the 1980 tour.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six of eight recordings are marked positive, praising standout songs (Ship Of Fools, Scarlet>Fire, Black Peter, Half‑Step sequence) and overall energy; two mixed reviews note minor vocal roughness but no consistent negative criticism. The strong consensus yields a solid positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent audience capture with full, crystal‑clear mix that highlights the inventive setlist pairings and gives the best overall listening experience.

key_highlights: Ripping “Mississippi Half-Step Uptown Toodeloo” → “Franklin’s Tower” opener
Scorching “Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain” sequence and heartfelt “Ship Of Fools”
Inventive B‑3 driven pairings: “1/2 Step > Franklin’s > Stranger” and “Don’t Ease Me In > Promised Land”
Strong “Black Peter” performance and seamless jam flow

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a heartfelt lead on Ship Of Fools and solid guitar throughout, though his vocals show strain during Mississippi Half‑Step.
band_performance.Bob: Provides steady rhythm work that underpins the jams; reviewers note his contributions as fine and appreciated.
band_performance.Brent: Tears into B‑3 on the 1/2 Step > Franklin’s > Stranger and Don’t Ease Me In > Promised Land pairings, adding bright keyboard texture.
